title: gnaphalium canescens subsp. beneolens
intro: Gnaphalium canescens subsp. beneolens, a lesser-known subspecies of the Gnaphalium canescens plant, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is also known as the "white cudweed" due to its white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Gnaphalium canescens subsp. beneolens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, fever, and digestive issues.
